questions.question: Explain the time complexity of the brute force approach for the Travelling Salesman Problem, which involves finding the shortest possible tour that visits each city and returns to the starting city. What assumptions are made to calculate this time complexity?
questions.hint: Consider the number of cities and the number of permutations of cities

questions.question: An instance of the Travelling Salesman Problem has 10 cities. If each city can be visited in any order, how many possible permutations of city orderings can be explored using a brute force approach?

Compare and contrast the time complexities of the brute force approach and the greedy algorithm for the Travelling Salesman Problem. Which approach is generally more efficient for large instances of the problem?
questions.hint: Consider the number of cities and the number of permutations of cities
